title: params.rs write is non-atomic: partial file on process-kill leaves cache poisoned
description: params.rs:245 std::fs::write(path, &data) writes the full ~48MB file non-atomically. If the process is killed mid-write, a partial file is left on disk. The next run will: read the file, fail the BLAKE2b check, delete it (line 225), then re-download. This self-heals but requires an unexpected extra network download that may confuse the user. Fix: write to a temp file in the same directory (e.g. path.with_extension('tmp')), verify hash on the temp file, then rename atomically. std::fs::rename is atomic on POSIX when src and dst are on the same filesystem.
